User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FinTrack Automation application. Stores basic profile information and links to organizations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and identification.

name.description: The full name of the user, if provided.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the user registered for the application.

organizationIds.description: References to organizations the user is associated with. (Relationship: User N:N Organization)

Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a business or entity whose financial data is managed by FinTrack Automation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The official name of the organization.

address.description: The physical address of the organization.

contactEmail.description: A general contact email for the organization.

userIds.description: References to users who belong to this organization. (Relationship: Organization N:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the organization record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of an organization, primarily used for invoice management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this customer belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Customer)

name.description: The full name or company name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

address.description: The billing or primary address of the customer.

phone.description: The phone number of the customer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was last updated.

FinancialAccount.title: FinancialAccount
FinancialAccount.description: Represents a financial account (e.g., bank account, credit card) associated with an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FinancialAccount entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this financial account belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N FinancialAccount)

name.description: A descriptive name for the account (e.g., 'Business Checking', 'Credit Card').

type.description: The type of financial account (e.g., 'Checking', 'Savings', 'Credit Card', 'Cash').

balance.description: The current balance of the account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the financial account record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the financial account record was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for financial transactions (e.g., 'Rent', 'Utilities', 'Salary').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this category belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Category)

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Office Supplies', 'Travel').

type.description: The type of financial flow this category represents (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense').

description.description: A detailed description of the category.

isSystemDefined.description: Indicates if the category is a default, system-provided category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category record was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial transaction, either an income or an expense.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the FinancialAccount from or to which the transaction occurred. (Relationship: FinancialAccount 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category assigned to this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

date.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

description.description: A brief description or memo for the transaction.

referenceNumber.description: An optional reference number, such as a check number or bank transaction ID.

isAutomated.description: Indicates if the transaction was automatically extracted and categorized.

documentId.description: Reference to the FinancialDocument from which this transaction was extracted, if applicable. (Relationship: FinancialDocument 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice created and managed by an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that issued this invoice. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Invoice)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who is the recipient of this invoice. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Invoice)

invoiceNumber.description: A human-readable, unique identifier for the invoice (e.g., INV-2023-001).

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total amount due for the invoice, sum of all invoice items.

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'Draft', 'Sent', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled').

notes.description: Additional notes or comments for the invoice.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice record was last updated.

InvoiceItem.title: InvoiceItem
InvoiceItem.description: Represents a single line item within an invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceItem entity.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice this item belongs to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N InvoiceItem)

description.description: A description of the service or product being invoiced.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

total.description: The total cost for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice item record was last updated.

FinancialDocument.title: FinancialDocument
FinancialDocument.description: Represents an uploaded financial document (e.g., receipt, bank statement) from which data can be extracted.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FinancialDocument entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this document. (Relationship: Organization 1:N FinancialDocument)

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N FinancialDocument)

documentType.description: The type of financial document (e.g., 'Receipt', 'Bank Statement', 'Bill').

originalFileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

storageUrl.description: The URL where the original document file is stored.

extractedData.description: JSON string containing the raw data extracted from the document by OCR/AI. The structure of this data can vary based on document type.

status.description: The processing status of the document (e.g., 'Uploaded', 'Processing', 'Processed', 'Error', 'Needs Review').

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

processedDate.description: The date and time when the document finished processing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the financial document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the financial document record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose UID matches the 'userId' path parameter. Each document's 'id' field MUST match the 'userId' path parameter. Security rules: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`

Root collection for organizations. Each document contains an `ownerId` and a `members` map (`{[userId]: "role"}`) for collaborative access control. This `members` map is denormalized into all subcollection documents for Authorization Independence.
Customers specific to an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'customerId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

definition.description: Financial accounts belonging to an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'financialAccountId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: financialAccountId
params.description: Unique identifier for the financial account.

definition.description: Transaction categories defined by an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'categoryId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

definition.description: Financial transactions recorded by an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'transactionId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

definition.description: Invoices issued by an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'invoiceId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

definition.description: Line items for a specific invoice. Includes denormalized `invoiceId`, `organizationId`, and `organizationMembers` map for Authorization Independence. The 'id' field MUST match the 'invoiceItemId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the grandparent organization.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent invoice.

params.name: invoiceItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the invoice item.

definition.description: Uploaded financial documents for an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId`, `organizationMembers` map, and `uploadedByUserId` for Authorization Independence and user attribution. The 'id' field MUST match the 'financialDocumentId' path parameter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: financialDocumentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the financial document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. The foundation is a top-level `/users` collection for individual user profiles, followed by a `/organizations` collection that acts as the root for all collaborative data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design prioritizes Authorization Independence by implementing extensive denormalization for collaborative data. For the `/users/{userId}` collection, ownership is inherently path-based (`request.auth.uid == userId`), ensuring atomic and independent authorization.

For all organization-related data, which is collaborative, the `Organization` document at `/organizations/{organizationId}` contains a `members` map (`{[userId]: "role"}`). To eliminate `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations (Strategy A), this `members` map (or a derivative containing the necessary authorization context) is **denormalized and copied into every document in its subcollections**. Specifically, each `Customer`, `FinancialAccount`, `Category`, `Transaction`, `Invoice`, `InvoiceItem`, and `FinancialDocument` document will include an `organizationId` field (to link back to the parent organization) and an `organizationMembers` map (a copy of the parent `organization`'s `members` map). This allows security rules to evaluate membership directly from the `resource.data.organizationMembers` field of the document being accessed, completely bypassing parent document reads for authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **`/users/{userId}`:** Access is strictly `request.auth.uid == userId`. A user can only access their own document, making `list` operations inherently secure (as they'd only ever query for their own known UID).
2.  **`/organizations/{organizationId}`:** Users can securely `list` organizations they are a member of by querying for documents where `members.{request.auth.uid}` exists. The security rule then validates this on the server side, ensuring only legitimate members are returned.
3.  **Subcollections under `organizations/{organizationId}`:** For collections like `customers`, `financialAccounts`, `transactions`, etc., the denormalized `organizationMembers` map within each document enables secure `list` operations. Clients can query for documents within a specific organization (e.g., `/organizations/{organizationId}/customers`) and the security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid in resource.data.organizationMembers`. This satisfies QAPs because the rule is a direct validation of access to a specific document, not an attempt to filter a potentially insecurely wide-ranging query.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
The design ensures that all documents within a given collection share the same security requirements. For example, all documents in `/organizations/{organizationId}/customers` are accessible to members of that specific organization. There are no mixed public/private documents within the same collection, simplifying security rules significantly.

**Access Modeling & Consistency (Standardization):**
*   **Private Data:** The `/users/{userId}` path uses path-based ownership for private user data.
*   **Collaborative Data:** All other entities are structured under `/organizations/{organizationId}`, where authorization is based on the denormalized `organizationMembers` map, providing a consistent pattern for multi-user access. The `ownerId` for an organization is also explicitly stored for clear ownership. Consistent naming like `organizationId` and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{organizationId}`, `{invoiceId}`) are used throughout.

This robust design ensures security by minimizing complex rule logic and leveraging a clear, predictable data hierarchy with explicit authorization context embedded where needed, allowing for straightforward and performant rule evaluation.
